mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to future earnings and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month FEDFUNDS trends are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in HOUST signals weakening housing investment and lower future household durables demand, reducing expected earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST contractions do not produce lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered periods with p <= 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, causing equity investors to mark down earnings expectations and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS is growing and UNRATE is falling, consumer demand is strengthening without labor stress, supporting revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises over 3 months and UNRATE falls over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-up and UNRATE-down signal has no positive average 20-day return premium versus periods with only one condition active.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in INDPRO signals expanding real output and operating leverage for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO 3-month trends have a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 for 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPIAUCSL combined with rising FEDFUNDS signals inflation pressure forcing tighter policy, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month inflation rate rises and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPI acceleration and FEDFUNDS rise signal does not underperform unconditional 60-day returns by at least 1 percentage point on average.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth signals broad real demand expansion that lifts aggregate sales and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth quarters are not followed by higher mean 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-positive growth quarters in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth with stable CPIAUCSL signals income-driven demand without an inflation shock, supporting earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average gain exceeds 150 thousand and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate does not rise
failure_condition: Reject if the joint strong-PAYEMS and stable-CPI signal produces non-positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns or no excess return versus strong PAYEMS alone.
